game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Doodle God™:** Players begin with four fundamental elements: Air, Earth, Fire, and Water, displayed in categorized lists on the user interface. The primary mechanic involves dragging one element from its category and dropping it onto another element to attempt a combination, with successful pairings creating a new, unique element (e.g., Water + Earth = Mud) that is then added to the player's available inventory. Progression is centered on discovering over 300 distinct elements, each expanding the player's creative possibilities and unlocking new categories for further experimentation. "Planet Mode" visually tracks the player's progress by animating discovered elements onto a growing planet, while "Mission" and "Puzzle" modes present specific creation objectives, such as crafting a "Locomotive" from a predefined set of starting elements. The game's core loop is a continuous cycle of combining existing elements to discover new ones, with the ultimate goal being to unlock the entire universe of available creations.
game_info.description: Discover the universe of creation! Start with four basic elements (Air, Earth, Fire, Water) and combine them to create new materials, objects, and concepts. Mix elements by selecting one with Arrow Keys and combining it with another using Space. Unlock categories like Life, Technology, and Magic as you progress. Your goal is to discover all 100+ unique elements and complete the creation tree!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate through available elements
Space: Select/Combine elements (select first element, then select second to combine)
Enter: Start game from title screen
Esc: Pause/Unpause game
R: Return to start screen

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic element combination mechanics and validates that new elements are successfully created and added to the inventory. This ensures the core gameplay loop functions correctly.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Systematically combines all available elements with each other in a methodical pattern. Starts with basic elements and progresses through newly discovered elements, checking that successful combinations produce new items.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The test should successfully create multiple new elements from the starting four. The inventory should grow, categories should unlock, and no errors should occur during combinations. Test validates inventory management and combination logic.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests the win condition by attempting to discover all available elements in the game. This validates that the game can be completed and the win state is properly triggered.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Uses an optimal combination strategy based on the element recipe tree. Intelligently selects element pairs that produce undiscovered elements, avoiding redundant combinations. Prioritizes unlocking new categories to access more combination possibilities.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The test should discover all elements and trigger the GAME_OVER_WIN state. The final score should reflect complete discovery. This validates the win condition logic and ensures the game is completable.

TEST_3.test_description: Tests UI navigation and element selection mechanics. Validates that the player can navigate through all categories and select elements correctly using keyboard controls.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Navigates through all available categories and elements using arrow keys. Tests boundary conditions (first/last elements, category transitions). Validates selection highlighting and category switching.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Navigation should work smoothly without errors. Selected elements should be clearly highlighted. Category transitions should be seamless. This validates the UI control system.
TEST_3.description: Tests UI navigation and element selection mechanics. Validates that the player can navigate through all categories and select elements correctly using keyboard controls.

TEST_3.expected: Navigation should work smoothly without errors. Selected elements should be clearly highlighted. Category transitions should be seamless. This validates the UI control system.
